summary: Large assembly-only repository-organization PR that split most of the sysdolphin text range out of the monolithic asm/text.s into per-module asm files and updated ldscript.lcf to link those objects in order. The diff is mostly movement of existing assembly, with asm/text.s shrinking by about 97k lines while new files such as asm/aobj.s, asm/cobj.s, asm/jobj.s, asm/pobj.s, asm/lobj.s, asm/pad.s, asm/video.s, and unknown-range code_8037*.s were added. There was no PR body or review discussion in the provided slice, so intent beyond the title and diff is inferred from the file split and linker changes.
what_changed: Created many new sysdolphin-oriented assembly files under asm/, each starting with .include "macros.inc" and a .text section declaration.
Moved the large sysdolphin tail of asm/text.s into separate object-level assembly files; asm/text.s shows 97,102 deleted lines and only a few retained/global-label additions.
Added named module files including aobj, cobj, displayfunc, fobj, fog, id, initialize, jobj, list, lobj, mobj, object, objalloc, pad, perf, pobj, video, and wobj.
Kept still-unknown contiguous ranges as address-named files: asm/code_80378A34.s, asm/code_8037AE34.s, and asm/code_8037E708.s.
Updated ldscript.lcf to insert a /* sysdolphin files */ block after text.o(.text), preserving explicit link order for the new objects.
Updated FORCEFILES in ldscript.lcf from { extab.o } to { extab.o object.o }, indicating object.o needed to be forced into the link.
Added .global directives in asm/text.s for labels such as lbl_80100480, lbl_8035C400, lbl_8035CE78, and lbl_8035D540, likely to keep references valid after the split.
smart_moves: The split grouped assembly by recognizable HAL/sysdolphin subsystem names instead of leaving the whole region in asm/text.s, making future decompilation work searchable and module-scoped.
The linker script listed every extracted object explicitly and in sequence, which is the key preservation mechanism when moving assembly without changing final layout.
Unknown or not-yet-understood regions were not overnamed; they were kept as code_80378A34, code_8037AE34, and code_8037E708, preserving address identity while still removing them from text.s.
The PR appears to be a mostly mechanical relocation with very little semantic editing, reducing the risk of introducing behavior changes during a preparatory split.
Adding needed .global labels in the remaining asm/text.s suggests the split accounted for cross-object symbol visibility rather than just copying text into new files.
decomp_lessons: When preparing a large monolithic assembly region for decompilation, first split by plausible library/module boundaries and update the linker script before attempting C translation.
Keep linker order explicit when extracting assembly objects; matching depends on object order as much as instruction bytes.
Use subsystem filenames where evidence is strong, but prefer address-based code_XXXXXXXX filenames for unresolved chunks to avoid misleading future contributors.
Cross-object labels that were formerly local within one giant assembly file may need .global exposure after a split.
A high-value preparatory PR can have almost no algorithmic code changes; the value is in creating stable module boundaries for later matching work.
naming_conventions: Sysdolphin module assembly files used concise lowercase subsystem names: aobj.s, cobj.s, fobj.s, jobj.s, lobj.s, mobj.s, pobj.s, wobj.s.
Unknown ranges used code_<start address>.s naming, e.g. code_80378A34.s, code_8037AE34.s, and code_8037E708.s.
Known exported SDK/HAL-style symbols retained HSD_* names where available, e.g. HSD_AObjInitAllocData, HSD_JObjLoadJoint, and HSD_JObjUnref in the excerpt.
Unidentified functions and labels retained address-based func_XXXXXXXX and lbl_XXXXXXXX names.
The linker script grouped these files under an explicit comment: /* sysdolphin files */.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Move assembly into separate .s files without changing instruction bodies, preserving address comments and existing labels.
Maintain every new object's placement in ldscript.lcf so the final .text layout remains stable.
Add object.o to FORCEFILES when a small extracted file might otherwise not be pulled into the link.
Use .include "macros.inc" consistently in each extracted assembly file.
Promote cross-file branch targets or referenced labels to .global when splitting them out of a monolithic assembly file.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, reviews, or PR body text were present in the provided context.
Because there is no human discussion in the slice, no reviewer intent or requested changes can be reliably extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that convert asm/aobj.s or asm/jobj.s into C after this split.
Search for later renames of code_80378A34.s, code_8037AE34.s, or code_8037E708.s once their module identities became known.
Check whether object.o remained in FORCEFILES after later decompilation of asm/object.s.
Look for build or linker fixes following this PR involving sysdolphin object order.
